title: Normal Operation / No Fault
shortDescription: One blink indicates normal operation with no stored fault codes.
fullDescription: A single LED blink on a Rheem or Ruud furnace control board indicates normal standby operation. The board has power, all safety circuits are satisfied, and no fault codes are stored. If the furnace is not heating, check the thermostat.
safetyWarning: Gas furnace safety: If you smell gas, leave immediately and call your gas company.

fixSteps.title: Check Thermostat
fixSteps.instruction: Verify thermostat is set to HEAT with temperature above room temp.

fixSteps.title: Raise Set Point
fixSteps.instruction: Increase thermostat 5 degrees above room temperature.

fixSteps.title: Check Batteries
fixSteps.instruction: Replace thermostat batteries if applicable.

fixSteps.title: Test Signal
fixSteps.instruction: Jump R and W terminals on control board to test for heat call.

whenToCallPro: If furnace does not start when thermostat calls for heat.

faq.a: No, on Rheem furnaces 1 blink means normal operation.

faq.q: Are Rheem and Ruud the same?
faq.a: Yes, Rheem and Ruud furnaces are made by the same manufacturer and use identical diagnostic codes.

faq.q: Why is my furnace not starting?
faq.a: Check thermostat settings, batteries, and wiring.

title: System Lockout — Retries Exceeded
shortDescription: The furnace locked out after failing to ignite within the allowed retries.
fullDescription: Two blinks on a Rheem/Ruud furnace indicate ignition lockout. The system tried to ignite the burners multiple times without detecting flame and has locked out for safety.
safetyWarning: Gas furnace safety: If you smell gas, leave immediately. Do not keep resetting if lockout recurs.

fixSteps.title: Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Cycle furnace power off for 30 seconds, then on.

fixSteps.title: Clean Flame Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Clean the flame sensor rod with fine emery cloth.

fixSteps.title: Check Igniter
fixSteps.instruction: Observe if igniter glows. Test resistance (10-200 ohms).

fixSteps.title: Verify Gas
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure gas shutoff valve is open and other appliances work.

whenToCallPro: If lockout persists after cleaning flame sensor and checking igniter.

faq.a: About 1 hour auto-reset, or cycle power manually.

faq.q: What is the most common fix?
faq.a: Cleaning the flame sensor resolves this issue most of the time.

faq.q: How do I find the flame sensor?
faq.a: It is a thin metal rod on a porcelain insulator in the burner area, with one wire attached.

title: Pressure Switch Error
shortDescription: The pressure switch did not close properly during startup.
fullDescription: Three blinks on a Rheem furnace indicate the pressure switch failed to close or opened during operation. This safety device ensures proper venting before and during combustion.
safetyWarning: Gas furnace safety: Never bypass the pressure switch. It prevents CO buildup in your home.

fixSteps.title: Check Vents
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect outdoor vent pipes for blockages.

fixSteps.title: Check Hose
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect pressure switch hose for cracks, water, or disconnection.

fixSteps.title: Clear Drain
fixSteps.instruction: Flush condensate drain on high-efficiency models.

fixSteps.title: Test Switch
fixSteps.instruction: Check continuity with inducer running.

whenToCallPro: If the cause cannot be found or the inducer motor is weak.

faq.a: Ice blocks vent pipes during cold weather.
Absolutely not. It prevents CO poisoning.

faq.q: How much is a pressure switch?
faq.a: $20-$50 for parts; $150-$300 installed.

title: Open High Limit Switch
shortDescription: The furnace overheated and the high limit switch tripped.
fullDescription: Four blinks on a Rheem/Ruud furnace indicate the high temperature limit switch opened due to overheating. Usually caused by restricted airflow.
safetyWarning: Gas furnace safety: Repeated overheating can crack the heat exchanger. Ensure CO detectors are working.

fixSteps.title: Replace Filter
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the air filter immediately.

fixSteps.title: Open Vents
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure all supply and return vents are open.

fixSteps.title: Check Blower
fixSteps.instruction: Set fan to ON at thermostat and verify blower runs.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Blower Wheel
fixSteps.instruction: Check for heavy dust buildup on the blower wheel.

whenToCallPro: If the filter is clean and vents are open but overheating continues.

faq.q: How often should I change the filter?
faq.a: Every 1-3 months for standard filters.
Yes, closing vents restricts airflow.
Yes, it can crack the heat exchanger over time.

title: Flame Without Gas Valve Command
shortDescription: Flame detected when gas valve should be off.
fullDescription: Five blinks indicate flame is sensed when the gas valve should be closed. This is a critical safety lockout condition.
safetyWarning: CRITICAL: May indicate a leaking gas valve. If you smell gas, evacuate and call 911.

fixSteps.title: Check for Gas
fixSteps.instruction: If you smell gas, evacuate and call 911.

fixSteps.title: Shut Down
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off power and gas supply.

fixSteps.title: Check Wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect flame sensor wire for damage or grounding.

fixSteps.title: Call Pro
fixSteps.instruction: Gas valve issues require professional service.

faq.a: Potentially yes. Have it inspected immediately.
No, gas valve issues need a professional.
$250-$700 for gas valve replacement.

title: Line Voltage Polarity Reversed
fullDescription: Six blinks indicate the Rheem furnace control board detected reversed polarity or a grounding issue in the power supply.
safetyWarning: Gas furnace safety: Turn off power at the breaker before checking wiring.

fixSteps.title: Kill Power
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker.

fixSteps.title: Check Polarity
fixSteps.instruction: Verify hot and neutral are correct at the furnace.

fixSteps.title: Check Ground
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure ground wire is secure.

fixSteps.title: Test Supply
fixSteps.instruction: Use outlet tester on furnace power source.

whenToCallPro: If not comfortable with electrical work.

faq.a: The flame sensor needs correct polarity to detect flame.
Common after installation or electrical work.
An outlet tester ($10-15) or multimeter.

title: Gas Valve or Igniter Circuit Error
shortDescription: Fault detected in the gas valve or igniter electrical circuit.
fullDescription: Seven blinks indicate an electrical fault in the gas valve or igniter circuit on a Rheem/Ruud furnace.
safetyWarning: Gas furnace safety: Do not manually operate the gas valve.

fixSteps.title: Check Igniter
fixSteps.instruction: Test igniter resistance (10-200 ohms is good).

fixSteps.title: Check Wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect all connections at igniter and gas valve.

fixSteps.title: Test Gas Valve Coil
fixSteps.instruction: Measure resistance across valve coil terminals.

fixSteps.title: Replace Component
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the failed component. Gas valve requires a professional.

faq.a: Yes, igniters are a common DIY repair.

faq.q: How much is a Rheem igniter?
faq.a: $20-$60 for the part.

faq.q: Could it be just a loose wire?
faq.a: Yes, always check connections first.

title: Igniter Circuit Open
shortDescription: Broken igniter element — open circuit detected.
fullDescription: Eight blinks indicate the hot surface igniter has an open circuit. The element is cracked or broken.
safetyWarning: Gas furnace safety: Turn off furnace before handling igniter. Do not touch element with bare hands.

fixSteps.title: Inspect
fixSteps.instruction: Check igniter for visible cracks.

fixSteps.title: Check Connection
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure wire connector is seated firmly.

fixSteps.title: Test
fixSteps.instruction: Measure resistance: good = 10-200 ohms, broken = OL.

fixSteps.title: Replace
fixSteps.instruction: Swap igniter. Handle by ceramic base only.

whenToCallPro: If new igniter does not resolve the issue.

faq.a: 3-7 years.
Use your model number at an HVAC parts supplier.
Skin oils cause premature failure.

title: Low Flame Signal
shortDescription: Flame sensor reading is below the minimum threshold.
fullDescription: Nine blinks indicate the flame sensor microamp reading is too low. The furnace may light briefly then shut down.
safetyWarning: Gas furnace safety: Weak combustion can produce CO. Ensure detectors are working.

fixSteps.title: Clean Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean flame sensor with emery cloth.

fixSteps.title: Check Insulator
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect for cracks in the ceramic insulator.

fixSteps.title: Watch Flames
fixSteps.instruction: Blue is normal; yellow means problems.

fixSteps.title: Gas Pressure
fixSteps.instruction: Have a tech check manifold pressure.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning does not help or flames are abnormal.

faq.a: 1.5-6 microamps.
At least annually.

faq.q: Can this cause high gas bills?
faq.a: Indirectly, from frequent restarts.

title: Rollout Switch Open
shortDescription: Flame rollout detected — flames escaping combustion chamber.
fullDescription: Ten blinks indicate the flame rollout safety switch has tripped. This is a serious condition usually caused by a blocked heat exchanger.
safetyWarning: CRITICAL: Flame rollout risks fire and CO exposure. Turn off furnace immediately and call a professional.

fixSteps.title: Shut Down
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off power and gas immediately.

fixSteps.title: Check CO
fixSteps.instruction: If CO alarm sounds, evacuate and call 911.

fixSteps.title: Check Exhaust
fixSteps.instruction: Look for vent blockages but do not restart.

fixSteps.title: Call Pro
fixSteps.instruction: Professional must inspect heat exchanger.

faq.a: Flames escaping the combustion area — fire and CO hazard.
Never without professional inspection first.

title: No Power to Control Board
shortDescription: No LED visible — control board has no power.
fullDescription: No LED on a Rheem furnace means the control board is not receiving power. Check breaker, switch, fuse, and transformer.
safetyWarning: Gas furnace safety: Turn off gas before troubleshooting electrical issues.

fixSteps.title: Check Breaker
fixSteps.instruction: Reset if tripped.

fixSteps.title: Check Switch
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure power switch is ON.

fixSteps.title: Check Fuse
fixSteps.instruction: Replace if blown (same amperage).

fixSteps.title: Test Transformer
fixSteps.instruction: Check for 24VAC output.

whenToCallPro: If breaker trips repeatedly or transformer failed.

faq.a: Usually a short in thermostat wiring.
Typically 3A or 5A.

faq.q: Can a bad thermostat cause this?
faq.a: Yes, a shorted thermostat can blow the fuse.

title: Rollout Switch Manual Reset
shortDescription: The rollout switch requires manual reset.
fullDescription: Eleven blinks on some Rheem models indicate the manual-reset rollout switch has tripped. A technician must inspect and reset.
safetyWarning: Gas furnace safety: If you smell gas, leave immediately and call your gas company.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off furnace power for 30 seconds, then restore.

fixSteps.title: Check Filter
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the air filter if dirty.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Check for loose connections.

fixSteps.title: Call Professional
fixSteps.instruction: If error persists, professional diagnosis is needed.

whenToCallPro: If basic troubleshooting does not resolve the error.

faq.a: The rollout switch requires manual reset.
It depends on the specific code. Some are minor and some need immediate attention.
$100-$500 depending on the issue.
